extra.reasoning: The property requires that globally, in every reachable state, opted_out implies not sent. Transition t5 (on send_attempt if opted_out and ticks >= 1: sent := true) is the hazard: it explicitly sets sent := true while opted_out is true, directly producing a state that violates the property. Execution: (1) optout fires t1 -> opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0. (2) tick fires t4 -> ticks=1, sent=false, opted_out still true. (3) send_attempt: guard (opted_out and ticks>=1) holds, so t5 is enabled and sets sent:=true while opted_out remains true. This reaches opted_out=true AND sent=true within 3 events (<=5), violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). Note that on this send_attempt both t3 (sent:=false) and t5 (sent:=true) are enabled simultaneously; since the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution and the rule set provides no explicit priority that disables t5, the t5 branch is a reachable execution exhibiting the violation. t5 contradicts the stated safety intent that no send should occur after opt-out.
